title: Vietnamese Springy Bun
description: A yeasty, springy Vietnamese dish featuring treacle and namul-blanch-season. Fodmap-friendly and adventurous in flavor.
instructions: Combine instant yeast, warm water, and 1 teaspoon of sugar in a bowl. Let the mixture sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
Add gluten-free flour, xanthan gum, and namul-blanch-season to the bowl. Mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until double in size.
Preheat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk, about 1/4 inch thick.
Brush the tops of the disks with coconut milk and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Cut each bun in half and fill with a fried egg, if desired.
Sprinkle with chopped Vietnamese mint and serve hot.
Note: Traditionally, buns are steamed instead of pan-fried, but pan-frying gives them a crispy bottom and a chewy interior that many people prefer.
